zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Sinclair of Roslin於大約1328年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安洛斯林時，她的父親William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn46歲，母親NN Rosabelle44歲。她於大約1353年6月3日與Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1418年在蘇格蘭享年92歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Sinclair of Roslin于大约1328年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安洛斯林时，她的父亲William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn46岁，母亲NN Rosabelle44岁。她于大约1353年6月3日与Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1418年在苏格兰享年92岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Roslin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern Wales): from a double diminutive of Rob or Rab, both pet forms of the Middle English personal name Robert , the latter with dialect unrounding of /-o-/.
French: from a shortened form of Robelin, a diminutive of the personal name Robert .

variants.name: Robert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Sinclair of Roslin was born about 1328, in Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland, her father, William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn, was 46 and her mother, NN Rosabelle, was 44. She married Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. about 3 June 1353, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died about 1418, in Scotland, at the age of 92, and was buried in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Sinclair of Roslin est née environ 1328, à Roslin, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn, avait 46 ans et sa mère, NN Rosabelle, avait 44 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. environ 3 juin 1353, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1418, à Écosse, à l’âge de 92 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Sinclair of Roslin um 1328 in Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn 46 und ihre Mutter NN Rosabelle 44. Sie hat um 3. Juni 1353 in Schottland, United Kingdom Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1418 im Alter von 92 Jahren in Scotland und wurde in Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Sinclair of Roslin è nata il circa 1328 nel Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland, sua padre, William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, NN Rosabelle, aveva 44 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. circa 3 giugno 1353, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1418, nel Scotland, all'età di 92 anni, e fu sepolta a Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Sinclair of Roslinが約1328年にスコットランドミドロスアンロスリンで生まれた時，父William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslynは46歳，母NN Rosabelleは44歳であった。約1353年6月3日にイギリススコットランドでThomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1418年にスコットランドで92歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Sinclair of Roslin(이)가 대략 1328년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 로슬린에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 NN Rosabelle(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 대략 1353년 6월 3일 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1418년 스컷랜드에서 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드에 묻혔다.

mg.about 1328–about 1418: tany ho any amin'ny 1328–tany ho any amin'ny 1418

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Sinclair of Roslin nasceu aproximadamente 1328, em Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland, seu pai, William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, NN Rosabelle, tinha 44 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. aproximadamente 3 de junho de 1353, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1418, em Scotland, com 92 anos, e foi sepultada em Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Sinclair of Roslin родилась около 1328 г. в местности рослинь, мидлотян, Шотландия, её отцу по имени William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали NN Rosabelle, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. около 3 июня 1353 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1418 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 92 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия, Великобритания.

sm.about 1328–about 1418: e tusa o le 1328–e tusa o le 1418

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Sinclair of Roslin nació aproximadamente 1328, en Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland, su papá, William Sinclair 8th Laird of Rosslyn, tenía 46 años y su mamá, NN Rosabelle, tenía 44 años. Se casó con Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. aproximadamente 3 de junio de 1353, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1418, en Scotland, a la edad de 92 años, y fue sepultada en Escocia, Reino Unido.
